_meta.title: Experiment Test Log
_meta.author: Caio, Claude (collaborative)
_meta.danilo_assessment: Template with 1 example row. Good schema concept but empty.
_meta.purpose: Chronological record of every experiment. This is the institutional memory of what we've tested, what we've learned, and why.

completed_tests.hypothesis: Higher price signals premium quality in marine plasma category
completed_tests.learning: In the marine plasma category, price INCREASES conversion (up to a point). $39 triggered 'too cheap to be real' skepticism for a product claiming 78 ocean minerals. Premium price anchored quality perception.
completed_tests.action_taken: Implemented $49 as base price. Queued PT-001 ($49 vs $59) to find ceiling.
completed_tests.statistical_note: DIALOGUE UPGRADE UPG-7: At ~2,500 total visitors and ~75 conversions (37-38 per variant), the +23% RPV lift is DIRECTIONAL, not definitive at 95% frequentist confidence. Standard sample size for detecting 23% relative effect at 3% CVR requires ~6,500/variant. PT-000 was large enough to detect the direction with high confidence, but the exact magnitude (+23%) has wide confidence intervals. Treat as 'very likely better' not 'precisely 23% better.'

active_tests.stakes: CRITICAL — entire M10 offer architecture assumes $59. If $49 wins, full revision needed.

planned_tests.description: Queue of tests prioritized by expected impact. Tests move to active_tests when launched.

queue.hypothesis: Problem-aware hooks ('Tired by 2pm') outperform ingredient-aware hooks ('78 minerals') on CTR
queue.control: '78 minerals your body is missing' — UGC format
queue.variant: 'Tired by 2pm? Your electrolytes are wrong.' — UGC format
queue.rationale: Validates whether ICP responds to problem framing or ingredient framing. Informs all creative direction.

queue.hypothesis: UGC format outperforms founder-to-camera format on ROAS for first purchase
queue.control: Founder (Caio/Danilo) to camera with same script
queue.rationale: Format determines production investment. If founder content works as well, saves $500-2K/month on UGC.

queue.hypothesis: Subscription-first default layout increases subscription rate by >15%
queue.control: One-time purchase as default, subscription as option
queue.variant: Subscription as default (pre-selected), one-time as downgrade
queue.primary_metric: Subscription rate (% of orders that are subscription)
queue.prerequisite: PT-001 complete (need stable base price first)
queue.rationale: Directly tests HYP-002 (subscription conversion target 50% → 60%). See also M10 PT-006.

queue.hypothesis: Biohacker/optimization audience converts at higher rate than general health-conscious audience
queue.variant: Narrow biohacker + optimization + fasting interest targeting
queue.prerequisite: CT-001 winner determined (need stable creative first)
queue.rationale: Validates ICP prioritization (M27). Biohackers are hypothesized as early adopters.

all_time.note: Extremely early — sample too small to draw conclusions about test program effectiveness.

knowledge_library.description: Accumulated learnings organized by domain. Updated after each test completion.

entries.finding: In marine plasma category, price increase from $39 to $49 INCREASED RPV by 23%. Price signals quality.
entries.implications: Sets floor at $49. Ceiling unknown (PT-001 testing $59). Category has strong price-quality association.
